skill.name: SEO Cannibalization Detector
skill.description: Analyzes multiple pages to identify keyword overlap and potential cannibalization issues with differentiation strategies
skill.summary: Detect SEO keyword cannibalization between pages and get actionable resolution strategies
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill with no executable code, network access, or file system operations. Static analysis scanned 0 files with a risk score of 0/100. The skill provides SEO cannibalization analysis guidance through natural language prompts only. No security concerns identified.

content.user_title: Detect and fix SEO keyword cannibalization issues
content.value_statement: Multiple pages competing for the same keywords hurts your search rankings. This skill analyzes content overlap and provides actionable strategies to resolve cannibalization.
actual_capabilities: Analyze keyword overlap between multiple pages
Identify title and meta description conflicts
Compare search intent across competing content
limitations: Requires manual input of page content for analysis
Cannot directly access Google Search Console data
Does not automatically implement recommended changes
Ranking data must be provided by the user

use_cases.title: Content Audit for Large Sites
use_cases.description: Identify cannibalization issues across hundreds of blog posts and product pages to improve overall search visibility.

use_cases.title: Pre-Publish Content Review
use_cases.description: Check new content against existing pages before publishing to prevent future cannibalization problems.

use_cases.title: Site Migration SEO Planning
use_cases.description: Analyze content overlap before restructuring site architecture to preserve search rankings during migration.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Cannibalization Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these two pages for keyword cannibalization: Page 1 [paste content/URL], Page 2 [paste content/URL]. Identify overlapping keywords and suggest which page should target each keyword.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick check between two competing pages

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Page Content Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: I have 5 pages targeting similar topics about [topic]. Here are the URLs and current rankings: [list pages]. Analyze keyword overlap, search intent differences, and recommend a consolidation or differentiation strategy for each page.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive audit of multiple competing pages

prompt_templates.title: Content Differentiation Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: These three pages all rank for '[primary keyword]' but serve different user needs. Analyze the current search intent for each and recommend specific content changes to differentiate their targeting while maintaining value.
prompt_templates.scenario: Refining existing content to reduce overlap

prompt_templates.title: Consolidation Strategy Development
prompt_templates.prompt: I have 4 thin pages competing for the same keyword cluster. Analyze the unique value in each page and create a consolidation plan: what to merge, what to redirect, and how to structure the new unified page for maximum search visibility.
prompt_templates.scenario: Merging weak competing pages into authoritative content

output_examples.input: Two blog posts targeting 'best running shoes' with similar rankings
output_examples.output: Cannibalization Report: Both pages target 'best running shoes' as primary keyword. Page A ranks #7, Page B ranks #12. Recommendation: Consolidate into single comprehensive guide, redirect Page B with 301, update internal links to point to merged content.

output_examples.input: Product category pages with overlapping descriptions
output_examples.output: Keyword Overlap Matrix: 65% content similarity detected. Differentiation strategy: Page A focuses on 'running shoes for beginners', Page B targets 'professional racing shoes'. Update titles, meta descriptions, and H1 tags to reflect distinct positioning.

best_practices: Assign one primary keyword per page in your content calendar
Regularly audit existing content before publishing new pages
Use canonical tags when consolidation is not feasible
anti_patterns: Creating multiple pages targeting identical keywords without distinct angles
Ignoring cannibalization signals in Google Search Console
Merging pages without implementing proper 301 redirects

faq.question: What is SEO keyword cannibalization?
faq.answer: Keyword cannibalization occurs when multiple pages on your site compete for the same search terms, causing them to split rankings and reducing overall visibility in search results.

faq.question: How do I know if I have cannibalization issues?
faq.answer: Check Google Search Console for multiple pages ranking for the same keyword, or use this skill to analyze content overlap between pages targeting similar topics.

faq.question: Should I delete or merge cannibalizing pages?
faq.answer: Merge pages when they cover the same topic with similar depth. Keep separate pages if they serve different search intents, but differentiate their content and keyword targeting clearly.

faq.question: How long does it take to see results after fixing cannibalization?
faq.answer: Typically 2-8 weeks for search engines to re-index and re-rank pages after implementing consolidation, redirects, or content differentiation changes.

faq.question: Can internal linking cause cannibalization?
faq.answer: Yes, using identical anchor text to link to different pages for the same keyword can confuse search engines about which page should rank. Vary anchor text to signal different page purposes.

faq.question: Is cannibalization always bad?
faq.answer: Not always. Multiple pages ranking for related keywords can dominate search results if they target different search intents. The issue arises when pages compete rather than complement each other.
